content.title: First Read Rule
body: If this is the agent's first time reading this README for the current work mission, read the entire README before opening deeper docs, following local routes, or relying on this corpus.

A first full pass is required once per current work mission, after a context reset, after a handoff or resume, or after the task materially changes. It is not required at every checkpoint in the same active mission.

After the first full pass, use the routing sections below to decide which local docs to reread or inspect next. At later checkpoints, reread only route-relevant sections unless the active `.core.*` route, roadmap, audit, or local stop condition requires a broader reread.
Do not treat this README as authority over App Behavior, architecture, implementation plans, proof, source, repo facts, product decisions, or explicit user instruction.
Purpose: Index for future UX schema system questions that should not block v1 adoption.
Audience: Agents and engineers extending the UX schema system after initial documentation.
Keep In Sync With: Decision records, schema files, generator work, and DB/admin UI implementation.
Update When: Open questions are answered, new questions appear, or future work is converted into durable docs.
Does Not Cover: Active product intent for any specific feature.

content.title: Open Questions
body: This folder captures future iteration notes that should not block the current UX schema system.
Files are prefixed in recommended reading order.

content.title: Routing
body: Use this area when a UX-system question is unresolved, deferred, or being considered for future iteration.
- Read future-iteration notes when deciding whether a question is still open, deferred, answered elsewhere, or ready for promotion into a decision artifact.
- Classify unresolved questions as open gaps, future scope, non-blocking follow-ups, or blockers according to the active roadmap or `.core.*` route.
Open questions are not App Behavior, product, source, architecture, implementation, or proof authority. They must not be used to invent behavior.
Stop when the agent can state the unresolved question, its current classification, what authority could resolve it, and whether it blocks the current task.
